prose.en: Configuration management practices are established and applied
Establish, test, deploy, and maintain hardened baselines that enforce the organization's cybersecurity policies and provide only essential capabilities (i.e., principle of least functionality)
Review all default configuration settings that may potentially impact cybersecurity when installing or upgrading software
Monitor implemented software for deviations from approved baselines

nist-withdrawal.label: PR.IP-01
nist-withdrawal.title: PR.IP-01
nist-withdrawal.prose: A baseline configuration of information technology/industrial control systems is created and maintained incorporating security principles (e.g. concept of least functionality)

nist-withdrawal.label: PR.IP-03
nist-withdrawal.title: PR.IP-03
nist-withdrawal.prose: Configuration change control processes are in place

nist-withdrawal.label: PR.PT-02
nist-withdrawal.title: PR.PT-02
nist-withdrawal.prose: Removable media is protected and its use restricted according to policy

nist-withdrawal.label: PR.PT-03
nist-withdrawal.title: PR.PT-03
nist-withdrawal.prose: The principle of least functionality is incorporated by configuring systems to provide only essential capabilities
